  • Clustering based on Assets/HH Characterisitcs
    • Using BIC To Determine Number of Clusters
    • Clustering Results

Wealth Indicators V2

Author

MK

This document is a summary of assets and household characteristics of the PATHOME study. Included are two methods of classifying households based on their assets: a Wealth Index, which is a numeric variable which summarizes each households assets, and a cluster number, which groups households into groups based on their assets. All data is from the Household Survey.

Wealth Index Calculation

The variables used to compute the wealth index are: income level and if they own: bike, motorcycle, car, fridge, smart phone, radio, internet, electric stove, computer, laundry machine, and dishwasher. Also, household characteristics are also taken into account. These variables include: if their own or rent their house, their house type, number of rooms in their house, their kitchen location, floor type, wall type, and if they have lockable gates, doors, or windows.

The wealth index was calculated by taking the first component of the PCA (values which summarize the most significant patters found among all the variables, in this case, wealth), and centering/scaling the values to be bounded by 0 and 10.

PCA Diagnositic Plots

  • Scree Plot
  • PCA Biplot: Dim-1 vs Dim-2 Without Transformation
  • Dim-1 Contribution

Adjusted Wealth Score

Removed any variables that may be tested for pathogen stuides.

  • Scree Plot
  • PCA Biplot: Dim-1 vs Dim-2 Without Transformation
  • Dim-1 Contribution

Wealth Score Vizualization

In general, a higher wealth score indicates a higher income/number of assets. Income, owning a computer, fridge, and internet have the most impact on the wealth score. Although is one of the highest drivers of the wealth index, other assets still have an impact, as can be seen in the following plot.

Table of Assets (and other important hh charactersitics) by Wealth Index Quartile

Adj_wealth_index
Mean (SD) 4.44 (1.55) 6.71 (0.381) 7.79 (0.259) 8.83 (0.472)
Median [Min, Max] 4.89 [0, 5.99] 6.67 [6.01, 7.27] 7.80 [7.30, 8.17] 8.81 [8.18, 10.0]

Clustering based on Assets/HH Characterisitcs

Variables for LCA: income, bike, motorcycle, car, fridge, smart_phone, tv, radio, internet, electric_stove, computer, house_type, own_rent, n_rooms, kitchen_loc, floor_type, wall_type, compound_ground, lockable_gate, flooding, water_source, no_HW_station, energy_source, locking_windows, locking_doors

Using BIC To Determine Number of Clusters

Clustering Results

The variables used for clustering were the same variables used for PCA to obtain the wealth index.

Looking at the breakdown of the Wealth Index across SES and Clusters, it looks as though the clustering is picking up on similar trends as the PCA. We see that cluster 1 has the “least” assets and cluster 3 has the “most” assets. This is good.

Cluster 1
(N=145)		 Cluster 2
(N=50)		 Cluster 3
(N=85)
